topic.what: Stringology-Based Cryptology (SBC) framework applying string processing algorithms to analyze structural properties of cryptographic outputs
topic.how: Interprets cryptographic sequences as symbolic strings; extracts substring frequency distributions, recurrence patterns, and entropy metrics using sliding-window pattern analysis across pattern lengths 8, 16, and 32 bits
topic.why_matters: Provides complementary perspective to traditional statistical randomness tests (NIST STS, TestU01, PractRand) by capturing localized structural patterns that may not be observable through global statistical analysis alone

characteristics.what_it_measures: Normalized pattern frequency in cipher-generated sequences
characteristics.context: Cipher outputs show higher normalized frequencies than random sequences across all pattern lengths tested

characteristics.what_it_measures: Absolute difference in pattern frequency distributions between cipher and random sequences
characteristics.context: Consistently non-zero deviation across pattern lengths indicates measurable structural differences

characteristics.what_itasures: Pattern entropy of substring distributions using Shannon entropy formula
characteristics.context: Cipher-generated sequences show slightly lower entropy than random, suggesting minor structural biases
Threat model defined but no empirical adversarial advantage reported; paper emphasizes complementary analysis not exploitation
